questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a defining characteristic of all living organisms?
questions.explanation: Growth and Reproduction are not absolute defining properties because non-living things (like mountains) grow and some living things (like mules) do not reproduce.

questions.text: The defining property of living organisms is:
questions.explanation: Ability to sense their surroundings and respond to environmental stimuli is unique to living beings.

questions.text: The process of naming organisms according to a standardized system is:
questions.explanation: Binomial nomenclature ensures every organism has one universal name.

questions.text: Binomial Nomenclature was introduced by:
questions.explanation: Linnaeus is considered the Father of Taxonomy.

questions.text: In Mangifera indica L., 'indica' represents:
questions.explanation: The first name is the genus and the second is the species (specific epithet).

questions.text: The correct sequence of taxonomic categories is:
options: Species → Genus → Family → Order → Class → Phylum → Kingdom
Kingdom → Phylum → Order → Class → Family → Genus → Species
Species → Genus → Order → Family → Class → Phylum → Kingdom
Kingdom → Class → Phylum → Order → Family → Genus → Species
questions.explanation: Taxonomic hierarchy is an ascending arrangement.

questions.text: The basic unit of classification is:
questions.explanation: A species is a group of individual organisms with fundamental similarities.

questions.text: Families are characterized on the basis of ________ of plant species.
questions.explanation: Both vegetative and floral characteristics are used for classification into families.

questions.text: A storehouse of collected plant specimens that are dried, pressed and preserved on sheets is:
questions.explanation: Herbarium sheets carry a label providing information about the specimen.

questions.text: The Indian Botanical Garden is located at:
questions.explanation: It is famous for the Great Banyan Tree.

questions.text: Taxonomic aid used for identification of plants and animals based on similarities and dissimilarities is:
questions.explanation: Keys are based on contrasting characters generally in a pair called couplet.

questions.text: ICBN stands for:
questions.explanation: It provides rules for naming plants.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a characteristic of life?
questions.explanation: Autotrophs (plants) do not consume other organisms.

questions.text: Genus Solanum includes which of the following?
questions.explanation: Solanum tuberosum (potato) and Solanum melongena (brinjal) belong to same genus.

questions.text: Felis is the genus of:
questions.explanation: Lion and Tiger belong to Panthera; Cat belongs to Felis.

questions.text: Solanaceae and Convolvulaceae are included in which order?
questions.explanation: Order Polymoniales is mainly based on floral characters.

questions.text: Human beings belong to the family:
questions.explanation: Mammalia is class, Primata is order, Chordata is phylum.

questions.text: Collections of preserved plant and animal specimens for study and reference are in:
questions.explanation: Museums often have skeletons and specimens in preservative solutions.

questions.text: Scientific names are generally in ________ and written in ________.
questions.explanation: Latin is used as it is a dead language and will not change.

questions.text: Which of the following contains information on any one taxon?
questions.explanation: 'Mono' means single.

questions.text: As we go from species to kingdom, the number of common characteristics:
questions.explanation: Lower the taxa, more are the characteristics that the members within the taxon share.

questions.text: Triticum aestivum is the scientific name of:
questions.explanation: Wheat belongs to family Poaceae.

questions.text: Systematics refers to the study of:
questions.explanation: It includes taxonomy plus evolutionary history.

questions.text: The sum total of all chemical reactions occurring in our body is:
questions.explanation: Defining feature of life.

questions.text: Herbarium label does NOT carry information on:
questions.explanation: Height is not a standard requirement for herbarium labels.

questions.text: National Botanical Research Institute (NBRI) is located at:
questions.explanation: Located in Uttar Pradesh, India.

questions.text: Which provides information for identification of names of species found in an area?
questions.explanation: Manuals are useful in providing information for identification.

questions.text: Panthera leo is the scientific name for:
questions.explanation: Leo for Lion, Tigris for Tiger, Pardus for Leopard.

questions.text: Biological name should be ________ when hand-written.
questions.explanation: Underlining indicates their Latin origin.

questions.text: Which of the following belongs to the same family?
questions.explanation: They all belong to Felidae.

questions.text: Metabolic reactions can be demonstrated outside the body in cell-free systems.
options: True, and they are living things
True, and they are living reactions
questions.explanation: Isolated metabolic reactions in vitro are not living things but are surely living reactions.

questions.text: The number of species that are known and described ranges between:
questions.explanation: This is according to current estimates.

questions.text: Division is a taxonomic category used for:
questions.explanation: Equivalent to Phylum in animal classification.

questions.text: The family name for Musca domestica is:
questions.explanation: Musca domestica is the Housefly.

questions.text: Order of Housefly is:
questions.explanation: Diptera is the order of flies.

questions.text: Royal Botanical Garden is situated in:
questions.explanation: The most famous botanical garden globally.

questions.text: Taxon represents a ________ of classification.
questions.explanation: Each category referred to as a unit of classification, in fact, represents a rank and is commonly termed as taxon.

questions.text: Which of the following represents the class of Mango?
questions.explanation: Sapindales is Order, Anacardiaceae is Family.

questions.text: Biological names are generally derived from which language?
questions.explanation: Latin was chosen for its stability.

questions.text: Species name starts with a ________ letter.
questions.explanation: Generic name starts with capital, specific epithet starts with small.
